experiment: known-class embedding census host-254 cap calibration
naive_cost: Restarting the 656-host census would discard 254 exact two-implementation results and repeat roughly 1.4 core-hours. Raising the cap globally without measurement could conceal a performance regression.
opportunities_considered: Preserve the verified 254-host prefix and replay only the first blocked host in an isolated copy.
Keep the proved source/complement indexing and existing bitset implementation; do not re-enumerate the prefix.
Retain the independent NetworkX VF2 stream as the semantic cross-check even though it is the bottleneck.
Use one host per segment so a failure cannot contaminate a later host or lose completed work.
Bind the copied prefix by checkpoint, progress, manifest, per-artifact, and protected-code hashes.
Use compressed per-host JSON artifacts and an append-only hash manifest rather than an unbounded aggregate file.
chosen_reductions: Copy the stopped v2 experiment to a new path, independently verify its exact 254-row prefix, and execute only host index 254 with the unchanged producer implementations under a new hash-locked 60-second aggregate cap. The stopped v2 paths remain read-only evidence.
expected_throughput_gain: Approximately 254x less repeated host work than restarting. The calibration is one bounded <=120-second segment; production continues only if exact stream agreement completes in <=45 seconds, leaving 33% headroom under the 60-second cap.
soundness_basis: The original prefix has sequential indices 0..253; checkpoint rows equal manifest rows; all gzip streams and artifact SHA-256 values verify; the checkpoint, progress, and manifest hashes are fixed below. The new host must pass exact agreement on embedding count, mapping stream, vector occurrences, unique-vector count, vector stream, and unique vectors.
remains_uncompressed: NetworkX still enumerates the complete host-254 VF2 mapping stream. The calibration does not prove later hosts finish within 60 seconds; every later segment retains the same cap and automatic correctness, throughput, artifact-growth, checkpoint, and decision-value gates.

decision_thresholds.continue_if: host 254 completes with exact dual-stream agreement in <=45 seconds; completed count increases exactly 254->255; the original prefix remains byte-identical
decision_thresholds.redirect_if: the 60-second gate fires, any stream differs, any prefix hash changes, or checkpoint/manifest coverage fails
